questions.q: What was the main purpose of the Putrakameshti sacrifice performed by Dasharatha?
options: To win a war against Ravana
To bring rain during a drought
questions.explanation: Dasharatha was childless and performed the Putrakameshti sacrifice specifically to obtain sons to continue his lineage.

questions.q: Which king of Ayodhya performed the Putrakameshti sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha, the ruler of Ayodhya, performed the Putrakameshti sacrifice because he had no children.

questions.q: What rose from the sacrificial fire during the Putrakameshti?
options: A divine being holding a vessel
questions.explanation: A divine being emerged from the sacrificial flames carrying a vessel of sacred payasam for the queens.

questions.q: What sacred food did the divine being bring for the queens?
questions.explanation: The divine being carried a vessel of sacred payasam, a sweet rice pudding, to be shared among Dasharatha's queens.

questions.q: How many sons were ultimately born to Dasharatha as a result of the sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Four sons were born to Dasharatha: Rama, Bharata, Lakshmana, and Shatrughna.

questions.q: Which of these was NOT one of the four sons born after the sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Sugriva was the Vanara king, not a son of Dasharatha; the four sons were Rama, Bharata, Lakshmana, and Shatrughna.

questions.q: Dasharatha's deepest sorrow before the sacrifice was that he had no what?
questions.explanation: Despite his great kingdom and wealth, Dasharatha grieved because he had no children to succeed him.

questions.q: In which kingdom was the Putrakameshti sacrifice performed?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha ruled Ayodhya, and the sacrifice was conducted there to grant him heirs.

questions.q: The Putrakameshti was a sacrifice involving what sacred element?
questions.explanation: The Putrakameshti was a great fire (yajna) sacrifice from whose flames a divine being arose.

questions.q: The eldest of the four sons born from the sacrifice was destined to be which avatar?
questions.explanation: Rama, the eldest son, was the seventh avatar of Vishnu, born to destroy the demon Ravana.

questions.q: Which sage is traditionally credited with advising Dasharatha to perform the Putrakameshti sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha advised Dasharatha, and the sage Rishyasringa was invited to officiate the sacrifice.

questions.q: Which sage was specially invited to conduct the Putrakameshti sacrifice?
questions.explanation: The sage Rishyasringa, known for his power to bring rain and blessings, was brought to perform the sacrifice.

questions.q: Before the Putrakameshti, Dasharatha first performed which great horse sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha first performed the Ashvamedha (horse sacrifice), after which the Putrakameshti was carried out for sons.

questions.q: The divine being who emerged from the fire is traditionally identified with which deity or aspect?
questions.explanation: The radiant figure rising from the flames is described as a being of the sacrifice (Yajna Purusha), associated with the fire god and the gods' boon.

questions.q: How many queens of Dasharatha received the sacred payasam?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha had three principal queens: Kausalya, Kaikeyi, and Sumitra, all of whom received portions of the payasam.

questions.q: Which queen was the mother of Rama, the eldest son?
questions.explanation: Kausalya, the chief queen, gave birth to Rama, the eldest of Dasharatha's sons.

questions.q: Which queen gave birth to the twins Lakshmana and Shatrughna?
questions.explanation: Sumitra, who received a double share of the payasam in tradition, gave birth to the twins Lakshmana and Shatrughna.

questions.q: Which queen was the mother of Bharata?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi, the youngest of the three queens, gave birth to Bharata.

questions.q: According to tradition, why did Sumitra bear two sons while the others bore one each?
options: She received shares from both Kausalya and Kaikeyi
questions.explanation: In the popular tradition, Sumitra received portions of payasam shared by Kausalya and Kaikeyi, and so bore two sons, the twins.

questions.q: The boon of sons granted through the sacrifice was ultimately connected to the gods' plan to defeat whom?
questions.explanation: The gods arranged the birth of Vishnu as Rama, through this sacrifice, in order to destroy the demon-king Ravana.

questions.q: The Putrakameshti is best classified as which kind of event in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The Putrakameshti is a yajna, a Vedic ritual fire sacrifice, performed for the specific desire of obtaining sons.

questions.q: Sage Rishyasringa was married to which princess, who is associated with his coming to Dasharatha's court?
questions.explanation: Rishyasringa was married to Shanta, who in tradition was a daughter connected to Dasharatha's family, linking the sage to Ayodhya.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, the divine boon of sons is set in motion when the gods approach which supreme deity to plan Ravana's downfall?
questions.explanation: The gods approach Brahma, and the plan leads to Vishnu agreeing to be born as Dasharatha's sons to slay Ravana.

questions.q: Sage Rishyasringa was the son of which sage, according to tradition?
questions.explanation: Rishyasringa was the son of the sage Vibhandaka and was raised in seclusion in the forest.

questions.q: Shanta, the wife of Rishyasringa, was the daughter of Dasharatha but had been given in adoption to which king?
questions.explanation: Shanta was adopted by King Romapada of Anga, and her marriage to Rishyasringa connected the sage to Dasharatha's family.

questions.q: Rishyasringa was first famously summoned to King Romapada's land of Anga to end which calamity?
questions.explanation: Rishyasringa's presence was sought in Anga to break a long drought, after which his power to grant blessings became renowned.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, who is described as having instructed or guided Dasharatha that performing the sacrifice with Rishyasringa would yield sons?
questions.explanation: The minister Sumantra recounted an old prophecy of the sage Sanatkumara that Rishyasringa would help Dasharatha obtain sons.

questions.q: In the divine plan, Vishnu's portion was divided so that the eldest son received the largest share. Which son received the greatest portion of divine essence?
questions.explanation: Rama, the eldest, received the largest portion of Vishnu's divine essence, making him the principal avatar.

questions.q: Approximately how long after the queens consumed the payasam were the sons born, according to tradition?
options: After the completion of the gestation (around a year/the twelfth month)
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that the sons were born after the full term, with the births described as occurring around the twelfth month following the sacrifice.

questions.q: The Putrakameshti episode appears in which book (Kanda) of the Valmiki Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The Putrakameshti and the birth of the four sons are narrated in the Bala Kanda, the first book of the Ramayana.
